author: diggyt
content:
https://bitsum.com/download-process-lasso/
Launch Fortnite and sit in the lobby. FortniteClient-Win64-Shipping.exe CPU Affinity -> Always -> Select CPU Affinity. CPU Priority -> Always, and set it to High go to Efficiency Mode -> Always -> OFF.

author: diggyt
content:
Open Command Prompt (Admin) and run these exact commands one by one:

    bcdedit /set useplatformclock no

        Why: This forces Windows to use the TSC (Time Stamp Counter) instead of the slower HPET. This is the single most important tweak for 0.1% low stability.

    bcdedit /set disabledynamictick yes

        Why: This stops the CPU from stopping its clock during idle moments. It keeps the CPU "awake" and ready to process a frame at all times.

    bcdedit /set tscsyncpolicy enhanced

        Why: Specifically for the 14900K’s hybrid architecture, this ensures all 24 cores are perfectly synchronized in time.

    bcdedit /set useplatformtick no

        Why: Prevents Windows from using an extra synthetic layer for time-keeping, further reducing kernel-level latency.

    bcdedit /set hypervisorlaunchtype off

        Why: This is the "Nuclear Option" to ensure VBS/Core Isolation is dead. If this is ON, your CPU is running in a "virtual bubble," adding ~5-10% latency to every frame.

author: diggyt
content:
🆕 
Run CMD as Admin: bcdedit /set tscsyncpolicy Enhanced (You did this).

The Add-on: bcdedit /set clockres 10000

Why: This sets the global kernel timer resolution to exactly 0.5ms (10,000 units). While tools like "Timer Resolution" do this in the app, setting it at the BCD level ensures it's active from the second you boot.
